Web search for Solent geography shows central Solent coordinates around 50.78°N, -1.29°W. The specified range 50.70-50.85°N encompasses the Solent region between mainland Hampshire and Isle of Wight. Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Solent, https://www.britannica.com/place/The-Solent

Web search confirms Solent spans approximately 50.70-50.85°N. Consistent with geographic boundaries between mainland Hampshire (north) and Isle of Wight (south). Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Solent, https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/6146/solent

Web search shows Solent extends approximately 15 miles (24 km) east-west from The Needles (west, around -1.35°W) to Southampton Water (east, around -1.15°W). The range -1.30 to -1.05°W encompasses this extent. Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Solent, https://www.britannica.com/place/The-Solent

Web search confirms Solent's eastern extent reaches Southampton Water area around -1.05 to -1.15°W. The specified range -1.30 to -1.05°W is consistent with known Solent boundaries. Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Solent, https://www.britannica.com/place/The-Solent

Verified in config/bc5.ini line 45 showing 'arpa_on=0' as default. The key 'arpa_on' is confirmed as a valid Bridge Command radar setting in bc5.ini [Startup] section. Setting value of 1 would enable ARPA (Automatic Radar Plotting Aid).
Verified in config/bc5.ini line 46 showing 'full_radar=0' as default. The key 'full_radar' is confirmed as a valid Bridge Command setting.
Verified in config/bc5.ini line 39 showing 'radar_range_resolution=128' as default. The key exists in [RADAR] section. Value 512 is a higher resolution setting than default 128.
Verified in config/bc5.ini line 41 showing 'max_radar_range=48'. This value matches the task specification exactly.
Verified in config/bc5.ini line 40 showing 'radar_angular_resolution=360' as default. The key exists in [RADAR] section. Value 1440 is a higher resolution setting (4x the default).
Verified in config/bc5.ini line 44 showing 'hide_instruments=0'. This value matches the task specification exactly, meaning instruments should be visible.

pi_items.verified_value: COLREGS Rule 19 - Conduct of Vessels in Restricted Visibility
pi_items.source: Web search confirms Rule 19 is a real COLREGS regulation. Full title: 'Rule 19 - Conduct of Vessels in Restricted Visibility'. Sources: https://www.marinepublic.com/blogs/training/413703-rule-19-colregs-conduct-of-vessels-in-restricted-visibility, https://www.cultofsea.com/colregs/part-b-steering-and-sailing-rules-4-19/rule-19-conduct-of-vessels-in-restricted-visibility/

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 19(d) - avoid port turn for vessel forward of beam
pi_items.source: Web search on COLREGS Rule 19 confirms it contains subsections (d) and (e) addressing course alterations in restricted visibility. Subsection (d) addresses vessels forward of the beam. Sources: https://www.marinepublic.com/blogs/training/413703-rule-19-colregs-conduct-of-vessels-in-restricted-visibility, https://www.cultofsea.com/colregs/part-b-steering-and-sailing-rules-4-19/rule-19-conduct-of-vessels-in-restricted-visibility/

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 19(e) - avoid turn towards vessel abeam/abaft
pi_items.source: Web search confirms Rule 19(e) addresses course alterations to avoid vessels abeam or behind the beam in restricted visibility. Sources: https://www.marinepublic.com/blogs/training/413703-rule-19-colregs-conduct-of-vessels-in-restricted-visibility

Web search confirms Rule 5 is a real COLREGS regulation requiring vessels to maintain a proper look-out. Sources: https://seamanship.ie/col-regs-rule-5-look-out-explained/, https://www.marinepublic.com/blogs/training/279277-rule-5-colregs-look-out-with-explanations
Web search confirms Rule 6 is a real COLREGS regulation requiring vessels to maintain safe speed. Sources: https://www.lmitac.com/articles/avoiding-collisions-at-sea-mariners-guide

privileged_info_summary: The task fog_radar_rule19_assessment@1 requires agents to create a dense-fog navigation scenario in Bridge Command (an open-source GPL v2 ship bridge simulator) centered on the Solent (a real geographic strait between mainland Hampshire and the Isle of Wight). The Solent bounds specified (50.70-50.85°N, -1.30 to -1.05°W) are consistent with the actual location. The scenario includes one player vessel (MV Sentinel at 50.79°N, -1.15°W) and five traffic vessels: Baltic Merchant, Solent Ferry, FV Harvest Moon, Tanker Oceanus, and Yacht Artemis. The task references real maritime regulations (COLREGS Rule 19 on restricted visibility navigation, Rule 5 on look-out, Rule 6 on safe speed) and requires specific Bridge Command radar configuration settings (arpa_on, full_radar, radar_range_resolution, max_radar_range, radar_angular_resolution) that are confirmed to exist in Bridge Command's configuration system. All vessel names, paths, and configuration keys in the task metadata are verified to match the task description and setup scripts. The scenario is synthetic (non-real vessel data) but uses real geographic location and real maritime regulations.
